channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: World Bank Delays KSh 78 Billion Emergency Loan to Kenya Amid Crisis Response
item.description: The World Bank has postponed a KSh 78 billion emergency loan to Kenya after the government failed to provide detailed plans on how the funds would be spent. The loan, part of the World Bank's Rapid Re...

item.title: Fitch Ratings Affirms Benue State's Credit Rating Stability
item.description: Fitch Ratings, a global credit rating agency, has confirmed that Benue State's long-term issuer default rating (IDR) remains at 'B' with a stable outlook. This affirmation comes after improved fiscal ...

item.title: Australia's housing market, US-Iran tensions and AI strategy dominate news agenda
item.description: The article highlights critical developments across multiple sectors. Key points include escalating US-Iran tensions over control of the Strait of Hormuz, with the US imposing a 20% tax on cargo passi...

item.title: Pakistan Condemns Houthi Attacks, Supports UN Peace Process in Yemen
item.description: Pakistan's Deputy Permanent Representative to the UN, Ambassador Usman Jadoon, strongly condemned the Houthi ballistic missile attacks on Saudi Arabia during an emergency UNSC meeting. The envoy empha...

item.title: Protesters Demand ICE Abolition After Fatal Shooting in Maine
item.description: Protesters in Biddeford, Maine, erupted in anger after a U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) officer fatally shot a 26-year-old Colombian man, sparking calls to abolish the agency. The inci...

item.title: Thai influencer Aon Somrutai's PH visit focuses on creative collaborations
item.description: Thai influencer Aon Somrutai recently made her first official visit to the Philippines, marking a significant milestone in her career. During her visit, she expressed interest in exploring creative pa...

item.title: KZN Education Spokesperson and Wife Clarify Leaked Video Allegations
item.description: KwaZulu-Natal Department of Education spokesperson Muzi Mahlambi and his wife, Umlazi District Director Metu Mahlambi, have issued a joint statement addressing a controversial leaked video circulating...

item.title: Plumbing Company Pleads Guilty to Safety Violations After Worker's Death in Trench Collapse
item.description: Three years after Liam Johnston was fatally buried in a trench collapse while working for Mr. Mike’s Plumbing, the company pleaded guilty to one of 11 Occupational Health and Safety (OHS) charges. The...

item.title: New Theory Advances Understanding of Dynamic Black Holes
item.description: Scientists have developed a groundbreaking framework that extends the laws of thermodynamics to real, evolving black holes rather than static ones. This advancement addresses limitations in Stephen Ha...

item.title: Oil Prices Surge to 2020 Levels as Analysts Predict Strait's Unlikely Return to Pre-Pandemic Norms
item.description: Oil prices experienced their most significant surge since 2020, driven by market speculation that the Strait's economic policies will not revert to pre-pandemic conditions. Analysts suggest that geopo...

item.title: Persistent UI Issues in Anthropic's Claude Product
item.description: The article discusses three unresolved user experience issues with Anthropic's Claude AI product, despite its technical excellence. The first bug involves inconsistent login verification processes whe...

item.title: Political mourning and tensions after Ann Widdecombe's murder
item.description: The article discusses the aftermath of Ann Widdecombe's murder, focusing on the political reactions and tensions within the UK Parliament. Following the tragic event, politicians, including Reform's d...

item.title: Alberta judge freezes $8.5 million in assets of separatist lawyer over alleged fund misuse
item.description: An Alberta judge has issued a Mareva injunction, temporarily freezing over $8.5 million in assets belonging to Jeffrey Rath, a prominent separatist and legal advocate, following allegations by the Tal...

item.title: Safaricom APN Settings: No Free Internet Access
item.description: The article explains that Safaricom's APN (Access Point Name) settings do not provide free internet access. It clarifies that any APN configuration, whether default or manually entered, routes data th...

item.title: Atiku Dismisses Claims Over Court Ruling on ADC Primaries
item.description: Former Vice President Atiku Abubakar has dismissed claims that a court ruling on the African Democratic Congress (ADC) leadership crisis invalidated the party’s primary elections and its candidates fo...

item.title: Lindsey Graham's Sister Completes Senate Service
item.description: The article discusses the conclusion of Senator Lindsey Graham's sister's term in the U.S. Senate, highlighting her legislative contributions and bipartisan efforts. It notes her role in key committee...

item.title: Stellenbosch International Chamber Music Festival Concludes with Mahler’s First Symphony Performance
item.description: The 21st Stellenbosch International Chamber Music Festival (SICMF) concluded with a standing-ovation performance of Gustav Mahler’s First Symphony, 'The Titan,' marking the end of a grueling 10-day mu...

item.title: Top Movies and TV Shows to Stream This Month
item.description: The article highlights a curated list of the best movies and TV shows available for streaming this month, emphasizing diverse genres and themes. It includes recommendations such as 'House of the Drago...

item.title: June inflation hits 4.4%, surpassing RBI target for first time in 17 months due to rising fuel prices and West Asia conflict
item.description: The June CPI inflation rate reached 4.4%, surpassing the Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) 4% target for the first time in 17 months. This spike is attributed to rising fuel prices and the ongoing conflic...

item.title: Sandiganbayan to Decide on Bonoan's Immunity Deal in Graft Case
item.description: The Office of the Ombudsman is pushing for the removal of former DPWH Secretary Manuel Bonoan as an accused in a graft case, seeking to amend charges to exclude him. The motion, scheduled for July 14,...
